archive_files.desc:
Compress multiple files or a directory into a .zip archive.
files_to_add parameter can be a single file path, folder path, or comma-separated list of paths.

extract_archive.desc: Decompress a .zip archive into the specified destination directory.

create_plan.desc:
Create a multi-step execution plan before starting a complex task.
ALWAYS call this first whenever a task requires more than one tool call.

Args:
    title    : Short name for the task (e.g. "Audit Python files").
    overview : One-paragraph description of the objective and approach.
    steps    : JSON array of step objects. Each object must have:
               - "description": what this step does (str)
               - "tool": the exact tool name that will be called (str)
               Example: [{"description": "List workspace files", "tool": "list_dir"},
                          {"description": "Count lines", "tool": "shell"}]

Creates two files in artifacts/:
    implementation_plan.md  — full plan reference
    tasks.md                — live progress checklist

update_task.desc:
Update the status of a step in tasks.md during execution.

Call with status='in_progress' when a step begins,
then 'done' or 'failed' when it finishes.

Args:
    step_number : 1-indexed step number matching the plan.
    status      : One of 'pending' | 'in_progress' | 'done' | 'failed'.

Checkbox legend in tasks.md:
    [ ] = pending   [/] = in_progress   [x] = done   [!] = failed

add_plan_note.desc:
Append an observation or finding to the Notes section of implementation_plan.md.
Use this to record important discoveries, errors encountered, or decisions made mid-task.

Args:
    note : The text to append (plain string, markdown supported).

read_plan.desc:
Read the current implementation_plan.md and tasks.md.
Use this to review the plan mid-task or to understand what has already been done.

check_resume.desc:
Check whether an incomplete multi-step task exists and can be resumed.
Call this at session start when the user asks to resume a previous task,
or when a task was interrupted.

Returns the full plan + current task progress if an unfinished plan is found,
or a 'nothing to resume' message otherwise.

sqlite_query.desc: Execute a query (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, CREATE) on a local SQLite database and return results.

base64_encode.desc: Encode a text string to Base64 format.

base64_decode.desc: Decode a Base64 encoded string back to plaintext.

get_env.desc: Retrieve the value of an environment variable. Returns error message if not set.

list_env.desc: List the names of all active environment variables.

read_file.desc: Read contents of a text file with optional line-range (1-indexed). Set end_line to -1 for end of file.

write_file.desc: Write/overwrite full contents of a file. Automatically creates parent directories if missing.

patch_file.desc: Perform a search-and-replace modification on a file. Finds search_text and replaces it with replace_text.

list_dir.desc: List directory contents including files and subdirectories with sizes/types.

find_files.desc: Find files matching a glob pattern recursively within a directory.

grep_search.desc: Search for text pattern in files (case-insensitive plain text search).

delete_file.desc: Delete a file from the filesystem.

make_directory.desc: Create a new directory (and any parent directories) if they do not exist.

move_file.desc: Move or rename a file/directory from source to destination.

file_info.desc: Retrieve detailed metadata about a file (size, creation/modification time, permissions).

file_hash.desc: Compute the cryptographic hash (checksum) of a file. Supported algorithms: md5, sha1, sha256.

file_diff.desc: Generate a unified line-by-line diff between two arbitrary text files.

bulk_replace.desc:
Search and replace a text string recursively across all matching files in a directory.
Useful for codebase-wide refactoring.

git_status.desc: Get the current state of the git repository (modified files, untracked files).

git_diff.desc: Show changes made to files in the repository. Optionally specify a file path.

git_add.desc: Stage modified or new files to git index. Set files to '.' to add all changes.

git_commit.desc: Commit staged changes to the repository with a commit message.

git_log.desc: View recent commits in git history. Defaults to showing last 10 commits.

git_checkout.desc: Checkout a branch or restore files from git index.

python_interpreter.desc:
Execute Python code in a stateful, persistent dictionary scope and return stdout/stderr or execution errors.
Variable bindings, functions, and imports persist across multiple runs.
Use ask_user(prompt) to prompt the user for input.

ping_host.desc: Verify if a remote host is reachable (resolves IP and tests connectivity).

get_ip_addresses.desc: Retrieve the local and public IP addresses of this host.

download_file.desc: Download a file from a URL directly to the destination path.

http_request.desc:
Execute a raw HTTP request (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) with optional headers and body.
headers parameter must be a JSON string of key-value pairs.

scan_ports.desc:
Scan TCP ports on a host to check if they are open.
ports parameter must be a comma-separated list of port numbers (e.g. '22,80,443,8080').

dns_lookup.desc: Resolve IP addresses associated with a domain name.

create_pipeline.desc:
Creates or updates a reusable pipeline.
id: Unique ID for the pipeline (e.g. 'compile_and_test').
name: Human-readable name (e.g. 'Build & Run Tests').
description: Simple description of what it does.
definition_json: JSON string defining variables and steps.
  Example structure:
  {
    "variables": { "branch": "main", "retries": 1 },
    "steps": [
      {
        "id": "fetch_code",
        "name": "Fetch Code",
        "type": "shell",
        "action": "git pull origin {{variables.branch}}"
      },
      {
        "id": "run_tests",
        "name": "Run unit tests",
        "type": "shell",
        "action": "pytest",
        "depends_on": ["fetch_code"]
      }
    ]
  }

run_pipeline.desc:
Triggers and executes a pipeline DAG run asynchronously.
pipeline_id: ID of the pipeline to run.
inputs_json: JSON string of input variable values.

cancel_pipeline.desc:
Cancels/aborts an active pipeline run and kills any executing child processes.
run_id: ID of the pipeline run.

get_pipeline_run_logs.desc:
Retrieves step-by-step logs and output state for a specific pipeline run.
run_id: ID of the pipeline run.

list_processes.desc: List running system processes (PID, Name). Optional filter_name to narrow down.

kill_process.desc: Terminate a process by its PID.

run_background_command.desc: Run a shell command asynchronously in the background. Returns the process PID.

get_background_output.desc: Get the latest stdout/stderr logs of a running background process using its PID.

system_resources.desc: Retrieve system storage (disk usage) and memory availability info.

create_routine.desc:
Creates a new routine (scheduled task).
name: Unique name for the routine.
schedule: Cron expression (e.g. '*/5 * * * *') or interval (e.g. '10s', '5m', '1h').
type: Type of action, either 'shell' (to run a terminal command) or 'prompt' (to run an agent prompt).
action: The CLI command or the agent prompt to run.
timeout: Maximum execution timeout in seconds.

list_routines.desc: Lists all configured routines, their schedules, types, statuses, and next run times.

pause_routine.desc: Pauses an active routine so it will not be executed by the scheduler.

resume_routine.desc: Resumes a paused routine, recalculating its next run time.

delete_routine.desc: Deletes a routine and its execution history logs.

view_routine_logs.desc: Retrieves the recent execution logs for a specific routine.

web_search.desc: Search the web for the given query using DuckDuckGo HTML search. Returns titles, links, and snippets of results.

synthesized_test_tool.desc: This is a dynamically created test tool.

system_info.desc: Retrieve details about the current operating system, machine specifications, Python version, and working directory.

read_tool_source.desc:
Read the source code of an existing on-demand tool by name.
Use this before upgrading a tool so you can see what it currently does
and write an expanded version that preserves existing behaviour.
Returns the full source code string, or an error if the tool does not exist.

create_tool.desc:
Dynamically compiles, writes, and registers a new tool function.
Automatically checks and installs missing Python dependencies before writing any code.
The code MUST define a function decorated with @tool (imported from registry).
Example:
    from registry import tool
    @tool
    def my_tool(arg: str):
        """Does something useful."""
        return "Result: " + arg

upgrade_tool.desc:
Upgrade an existing on-demand tool with expanded capabilities.
Use this when a user request is a near-match for an existing tool but the tool
needs new parameters, logic, or library support to fully cover the task.
Workflow:
  1. Call read_tool_source(name) to inspect the current implementation.
  2. Write upgraded_code that preserves all existing behaviour AND adds the new capability.
  3. Call upgrade_tool(name, upgraded_code) — it backs up the original, validates,
     installs new deps, overwrites, and hot-reloads the tool live.
Only upgrades tools that exist on disk (on-demand tools). Built-in tools cannot be upgraded.

take_screenshot.desc: Capture a screenshot of the entire primary screen and save it as screenshot.png.

fetch_url.desc: Fetch the text content of a webpage (HTML converted to simple plain text).
